steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says writers often struggle with follow-up novels, but Tommy Orange's follow-up elevated his reputation. The transition must signal that his case is an exception to the general pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Specifically,: Frames Orange as a confirming example of the struggle pattern, contradicting the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — Accordingly,: Implies his result follows logically from the general rule — the opposite of what the passage states. ✗
  • Choice C — Sometimes,: Signals that while follow-ups often go poorly, Orange's case is a notable exception. ✓
  • Choice D — For example,: Like 'Specifically,' it treats his case as a typical illustration rather than an exception. ✗
C is correct.
